title: Undercut vulnerability proxy
summary: Estimate whether the driver is vulnerable to competitors pitting earlier by comparing own tyre pressure to peer fresh-tyre and pace signals.
rationale: External F1 strategy explanations emphasize undercut dynamics from fresh tyres and tyre degradation. The run’s peer and fresh-stop features point to a compact undercut proxy as a natural combination.
implementation_hint: Create a small block combining own old-for-compound or current-window pressure, positive pace loss, position percentile, and leave-one-out peer fresh-tyre or current-pit rates. Add a single undercut-vulnerability score and binary high-vulnerability flag.
expected_effect: Small ROC AUC gain with low-medium confidence.
risk: No explicit time gaps are available, so this is an approximation and may be race-dependent.
